data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI down 0.4%; Top stock is JMH USD
data.description:
JMH USD is the top stock with a 1.27% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 3,581.310 down 0.4% on Thursday, 10 October, at 5:21 p.m.

JMH USD is the most active stock, followed by SATS (0.80%) and Wilmar Intl (0.60%).

Venture and HongkongLand USD were at the foot.

data.title: COTD: SMEs lag behind larger firms in decarbonisation progress
data.description:
Only 34% of SMEs have made significant decarbonisation progress.

In Singapore, non-SMEs lead in decarbonisation with 82% already in advanced and mid stages, according to Bain & Company.

In contrast, only 34% of SMEs have made significant progress in their decarbonisation journey.

Smaller SMEs, or those with annual revenues below $10m are lagging behind, with only 23% making progress, compared to 61% of larger SMEs or those with revenues over $50m.

Meanwhile, for SMEs earning between $10m and $50m, 42% have made significant progress.

In addition, a third, or 33% of SMEs have yet to start their decarbonisation journey, whilst 20% are just beginning and 12% are at the basic stage.

data.title: Singaporeans unclear on palliative care’s role beyond end-of-life care
data.description:
Only three in 10 have a clear understanding of what it entails.

Although seven in ten Singaporeans are aware of palliative care, only 31% have a clear understanding of what it entails, according to a survey by Lien Foundation. Additionally, 39% said they know little about it.

The main misconceptions surrounding palliative care are that it’s only for those in the final six months of life, solely for the dying, and that choosing it means giving up curative treatment.

Though 80% of respondents recognise that palliative care aims to improve quality of life, only 38% are aware that it extends beyond end-of-life care, and 44% don’t know it can be combined with curative treatments.

Seven in 10 Singaporeans believe palliative care offers emotional support, whilst 70% recognise its value for families and caregivers.

The survey also found that whilst 90% would consider palliative care for themselves or loved ones, many would wait until a late stage of illness, citing concerns about focusing on treatment or high costs.

The result reflects the situation in Singapore, where palliative care is generally accessed late, with a median duration of 22 days.

Non-cancer patients typically receive even less support, averaging just nine days compared to 33 days for cancer patients.

Although there are challenges, 71% of respondents who had used palliative care rated the service positively, with 64% reporting it as moderately to highly accessible in Singapore.

data.title: Surbana Jurong and Hyundai partner to develop Small Modular Reactors
data.description:
They will also explore projects focused on renewable energy.

Surbana Jurong and Hyundai Engineering & Construction (HDEC) have partnered to explore opportunities in renewable energy, construction technology, and infrastructure development for Small Modular Reactors (SMR).

Under the Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), the parties will collaborate in four areas: exploration of potential projects, research and development, infrastructure technology, and SMR.

The research and development component will prioritise low-carbon business models and construction innovations, such as Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS) and Prefabricated Prefinished Volumetric Construction (PPVC).

On the exploration front, HDEC and Surbana Jurong will jointly explore potential projects focused on renewable energy and infrastructure development.

data.title: 6 in 10 investors prioritise sustainability in their portfolios
data.description:
They're keen despite recognising potential trade-offs.

Six in 10 investors in Singapore have added sustainable thematic investments to their portfolios, Fidelity International reported.

This reflects their growing commitment to responsible investing and a desire to make a positive impact, with the same proportion stating they want their investments to contribute to positive global change.

Whilst many embrace sustainable investing, half of the investors are aware of potential trade-offs, including the risk of lower returns or fewer available investment options.

data.title: Fullerton sells 1.21 million shares in ST Engineering for $5.65m
data.description:
Post divestment, Fullerton's interest stood at 0.1713%.

Temasek's Fullerton disposed of 1,210,800 shares in ST Engineering for $5.65m. 

Fullerton divested the share through a market transaction.

Post divestment, Fullerton's interest stood at 0.1713%.

Meanwhile, Temasek's direct and deemed interest in ST Engineering fell to 50.97% from 515.

data.title: SGX securities trading turnover hits $30.4b, up 75% YoY
data.description:
The bourse attributed its gains to the easing of monetary policies.

The Singapore Exchange (SGX Group) saw significant growth in its securities trading activity in September, recording a market turnover value of $30.4b, a 75% increase from last year.

Its securities daily average value (SDAV) also saw a surge, jumping 67% YoY to $1.45b.

According to SGX, these are the highest figures for both metrics since May 2022.

SGX attributed its gains to the easing of monetary policies and optimism fueled by China’s stimulus package, which drove portfolio repositioning.

"With China’s economic outlook still driving regional volatility, SGX Group remained the venue of choice for market participants managing Asia exposures with unrivalled efficiency," SGX said.

These factors also helped boost the bourse's derivatives traded volume to 28.9 million contracts, a 35% increase from September 2023.

In September, SGX also achieved single-day records in derivatives trading volume on two consecutive days, with the highest volume reaching 3.46 million contracts across equities, foreign exchange (FX), and commodities on 25 September.
 
Meanwhile, the daily average volume of derivatives (DAV) increased by 35% YoY to 1.45 million contracts.

Each measure reached its highest level in four and a half years in September.

In the same month, the bourse hit an all-time high for its total FX futures traded volume, climbing 35% YoY to 5.5 million contracts.

SGX INR/USD FX Futures led the increase, with volume up 55% YoY.  SGX USD/CNH FX Futures volume also increased, rising 12% YoY to US$17.4b contracts.

Within commodity derivatives, the increase was driven by SGX SICOM rubber derivatives and iron ore.

SICOM rubber hit a record 421,113 lots, with a single-day option volume high on 30 September as TSR20 prices rose. 

Petrochemical derivatives reached a three-year high with a monthly average open interest (OI) of 2.56 million metric tonnes. 

Iron ore derivatives also set a single-day record of 597,467 lots and a new monthly average OI of 3,178,291 lots. 

Overall, total commodity derivatives volume increased 23% YoY to 18.1 million contracts.

data.title: HDB issues $500m notes due 2034 at a 2.699% fixed rate
data.description:
The notes are payable semi-annually in arrears.

The Housing and Development Board (HDB) issued $500m worth of 10-year fixed-rate notes, due on 9 October 2034, at a rate of 2.699%.

The notes are payable semi-annually in arrears.

HDB said the notes are under its $32b Multicurrency Medium Term Note (MTN) Programme.

HDB issued the notes in denominations of $250,000 by way of placement to institutional investors.

data.title: HDB offers 8,573 flats under new classification framework in October BTO
data.description:
These flats will make up over 40% of HDB’s new flat supply in 2024.

The Housing and Development Board (HDB) will offer 8,573 flats in 15 projects under the New Flat Classification Framework during the October 2024 Build-To-Order (BTO) exercise.

These flats, classified as Standard, Plus, and Prime, will account for over 40% of HDB’s new flat supply for the year.

The 15 projects will be in Ang Mo Kio, Bedok, Bukit Batok, Jurong West, Geylang, Kallang/Whampoa, Pasir Ris, Sengkang, and Woodlands.

The new classification framework will categorize flats based on proximity to the city centre, transport links, and access to amenities, including social and commercial services in town centres.

data.title: Trek Technology to acquire 20% stake in China Chips Star Semiconductors
data.description:
The chip company recorded $4.44m in revenue with net assets of $4.11m.

Trek Technology will acquire a 20% stake in China Chips Star Semiconductors Co. Ltd. (CCSS) for a consideration of $2.09m.

This equates to 2,750,000 ordinary shares. Following the acquisition, Trek Technology may nominate representatives to the CCSS board, with no more than one-third of the directors.

Founded in 2022 and based in Shenzhen, with branches in Hong Kong and Nanjing, CCSS provides storage solutions, catering primarily to the telecommunications and electronics sectors in China. Despite being a relatively new company, CCSS has generated $4.44m in revenue and reported net assets of $4.11m  as of 31 July.

This investment will enhance Trek Technology's market presence in China, aligning with its expansion plans.
